title: quercus trojana subsp. euboica
intro: Quercus trojana subsp. euboica is a subspecies of the Oriental oak tree (Quercus trojana), native to Greece, particularly on the island of Euboea. It is a tree species within the Quercus genus, known for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The leaves and bark of Quercus trojana subsp. euboica have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems. The plant is prepared through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
